task_completion.description: Open the 'Indoor Area Camera' in the Nx Witness client.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Nx Witness application is open and 'Indoor Area Camera' is actively displayed in the main viewing grid.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the crowd dispersal event in the video footage.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The video timeline is positioned at or near the 60.0-second (1:00) mark, and the camera view shows people running or dispersing.

task_completion.description: Create a bookmark named 'Crowd Incident' covering the event.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A bookmark dialog or the Bookmarks panel is visible showing a bookmark explicitly named 'Crowd Incident' located around the 1:00 timeline mark.

task_completion.description: Create or open the safety report file at /home/ga/crowd_report.txt.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., gedit, nano) is open with the file path '/home/ga/crowd_report.txt' visible in the title bar, header, or save dialog.

task_completion.description: Document the behavior change timestamp and crowd dynamics (number of people, trigger, reactions).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text editor contains written content noting the timestamp (around 60 seconds / 1:00), an estimate of the number of people, the trigger for the panic, and the crowd's reactions.

integrity.description: The agent used the Nx Witness GUI to view the video and create the bookmark.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Nx Witness interface being actively interacted with (e.g., timeline navigation, bookmark creation dialogs) rather than API calls or terminal commands.

integrity.description: The agent used a text editor to write the report.
integrity.visual_evidence: A text editor interface is visibly used to type the report, rather than a single terminal command (like echo) bypassing manual entry.

integrity.description: The agent actually viewed the footage to determine the timestamp before writing the report.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the video playing at the specific event timestamp (around 60s) prior to or during the drafting of the report.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target camera is 'Indoor Area Camera' playing 'umn_crowd.mp4'. The video contains a normal crowd behavior that transitions to a panic dispersal event exactly at the 60.0-second (1:00) mark. The expected bookmark name is exactly 'Crowd Incident'. The safety report must be saved to the exact path '/home/ga/crowd_report.txt'.
